does: This paper introduces HealthBench Professional, a benchmark dataset designed to evaluate large language models on authentic clinical tasks that physicians actually bring to ChatGPT in practice. The benchmark comprises real clinician-authored conversations organized around three central use cases: care consult, writing and documentation, and medical research. Each example is scored using physician-written rubrics that were iteratively refined through adjudication by three or more physicians across three evaluation phases. The dataset was deliberately constructed to be challenging for current frontier models by enriching difficult examples at approximately 3.5 times their representation in the original candidate pool of 15,079 examples, and includes adversarial testing scenarios from about one-third of examples to probe model limitations and failure modes.
connects: This work extends prior efforts to benchmark medical AI systems, building on earlier evaluation frameworks that assessed language models on standardized medical knowledge tests and clinical reasoning tasks. HealthBench Professional moves beyond previous benchmarks by grounding evaluation in actual clinician workflows and real use cases rather than curated exam questions or synthetic prompts. It responds to the gap identified by the growing but understudied phenomenon of clinicians using general-purpose language models like ChatGPT for genuine clinical work, and combines evaluation methodologies from medical education assessment with modern LLM benchmarking practices that emphasize adversarial examples and difficulty-weighted sampling to track progress on frontier models.
enables: This benchmark enables rigorous measurement of how well large language models support authentic clinical work, allowing researchers and developers to systematically improve model performance on tasks that directly impact clinical practice. By establishing baselines on real clinician use cases, the work creates infrastructure for evaluating future models on their ability to safely and accurately assist with patient care decisions, clinical documentation, and evidence synthesis. The difficulty-enriched dataset and adversarial examples highlight failure modes that future research can target, potentially driving development of specialized or instruction-tuned medical models. Additionally, the benchmark provides a foundation for studying human-AI collaboration in clinical settings and can inform development of safety measures and guardrails for deploying language models in healthcare contexts.
